title: practical english usage - Part 29
content:
For details, see the following entries.

There are some grammatical complications, particularly as regards have.

I am being served. Have you had an invitation?  What do you do?

verbs. For this reason, each of them can appear twice in a single phrase.

passives. Be can also be a linking verb, and have and do can be ordinary full questions, negatives, emphatic forms, perfect and progressive tenses, and These three verbs live a double life. They  can be auxiliary verbs used to form INTRODUCTION Section 2  Be, have and do grammar •  17   be: general Don't be silly! Don't be such a nuisance!

somebody not to do something).

But do is used to make negative imperative sentences with be (when we  tell not I'm not often sick, (     J-don't pften-be-sick.) Normally, be is used without the auxiliary do.

Note the difference between He's being sick (BrE =  He's vomiting - bringing food not I was depressed when you phoned, (     7 was being-depressed...) not - I'm happy at the moment, (     I'm being happy-at the momen t) Who's being a silly baby, then?

I was being very careful. (= I was doing something carefully.) - You're being stupid. (= You’ re doing stupid things.) and behaviour, but not usually to talk about feelings. Compare: We   can use this structure to talk about temporary situations to do with actions 7 am being / you are being, etc + adjective/noun 18  be:  progressive        form   s ___________________ For be + infinitive (e.g. She is to arrive tomorrow), ►    42.       For contractions (e.g. I’m,  isn't), ►  337.

For the structure there is, used to introduce indefinite subjects, ►    20.

Do be careful, (stronger than Be careful.) Don't be afraid.

emphatic imperatives (►   19).

Questions and negatives are formed without do, but do is used in negative and You're being stupid.

But they are possible in some cases (►   18).

It's cold today, (     It's being cold today.) Progressive forms of the linking verb be are not usual.

Andy was very tired.

This is difficult.

Be can also be a linking verb, used to join a subject to a complement.

I knew we were being watched.

The hospital is being rebuilt.

(►  57.2).

As an auxiliary verb, be can have progressive forms in passive structures We weren't told about the meeting.

(►   3, 57).

Be can be an auxiliary verb, used to form progressive and passive structures 17 be:  gen  eral   __ ____________ grammar •  20  there is There's no need to hurry - we've got plenty of time.

Do you think there's any use trying to explain?

Is there any point in talking about it again?

There's no sense in making him  angry.

Note also the structures with sense, point, use (►   373)  and need.

There's something wrong. Is there anything the matter?

Note the use of wrong and the matter (►  524).

There's something worrying me.

There was dancing in the streets.

Is there anybody at home?

There were no footsteps to be seen.

There are some people outside.

pronouns like somebody, nothing.

no article, or indefinite determiners like some, any, no; and with indefinite We   use there in this way particularly with subjects that have indefinite articles, 2 indefinite       subjects There's some grapes in the fridge, if you're still hungry.

There's two men at the door, Dad.

However, there's can begin sentences with plural subjects in informal speech.

(    .., how many people therels-...) I don't know how many people there are in the waiting room.

There are is used with plural subjects.

There is a lot of noise in the street, (     His a let of noise in the street.) It cannot be used in this way.

There's ice on the lake. (More natural than Ice is on the lake.) There's a hole in my sleeve. (More natural than A hole is in my sleeve.) the verb. Note the pronunciation of there: usually /ðə(r)/, not /ðeə(r)/.

usually use there as a kind of preparatory subject, and put the real subject after In sentences which  say that something exists (or does not exist) somewhere, we 1  use 20 there      is  ____               ___ For other auxiliary uses of do, ►    27.
